title: Our Final Predictions For Oscars 2026's Best Supporting Actor Lineup: Who Will Win?
text: Jacob Elordi (Frankenstein) is ranked fifth. Despite winning at the Critics' Choice Awards, Elordi faded after failing to secure wins at the Golden Globes, Actor Awards, or BAFTA. He's viewed as an unlikely victor. Benicio del Toro (One Battle After Another) is ranked fourth. Once a major contender with multiple precursor nominations, del Toro lost momentum throughout awards season without major wins. His co-star emerged as the stronger choice from their shared film. Stellan Skarsgård (Sentimental Value) is ranked third. The analyst previously backed Skarsgård but reconsidered after his BAFTA loss. His Golden Globe victory provides some support, and international Academy voters could favor him, though his chances remain moderate. Delroy Lindo (Sinners) is ranked second. A surprise nomination without precursor recognition, Lindo benefits from the film's strong reception. His decades-long career creates an opportunity for voters to honor both the performance and his overall body of work. Sean Penn (One Battle After Another) is the predicted winner. Penn claimed victories at the Actor Awards and BAFTA. With three Oscar wins already, his industry standing and acclaim for this performance position him as the frontrunner entering the ceremony.
